failure_surface: Formulas written to target cells have syntax errors (double equals '==' instead of '=') and use identical static ranges for every row instead of producing meaningful per-row weighted averages
root_cause: Formula strings were constructed without proper validation before assignment, leading to duplicate '=' prefixes, and the loop logic applied the same fixed range ($C$3:$C$6) to all output rows rather than creating row-appropriate calculations
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Validate formula string construction to ensure exactly one '=' prefix, and implement row-specific range references or relative formulas where each output row should compute its own weighted average
evidence: Verification output shows 'J==SUMPRODUCT($C$3:$C$6,D3:D6)/SUM($C$3:$C$6)' with double equals signs making formulas syntactically invalid
All four rows (3,4,5,6) received identical formulas despite potentially needing different calculations per row
Code used ws[f'J{row}'] = f'=SUMPRODUCT...' which should produce single '=', indicating string handling corruption during construction

memory_items.title: Validate Formula String Construction
memory_items.lesson: When building formula strings programmatically, verify the resulting string contains exactly one '=' prefix and follows Excel formula syntax rules before assignment to cells
memory_items.generalization: Always inspect generated formula strings for syntax errors (extra symbols, missing operators, malformed range notation) prior to workbook save operations

memory_items.title: Use Appropriate Cell References for Row-Aware Calculations
memory_items.lesson: When writing formulas to multiple rows where each row needs independent calculation results, use relative references or construct row-specific range strings rather than applying identical absolute ranges to all rows
memory_items.generalization: For per-row computations in spreadsheets, determine whether each output cell requires distinct input ranges and adjust formula generation accordingly

memory_items.title: Match Output Range to Specification Exactly
memory_items.lesson: Ensure formulas are written to all cells within the specified answer position range, including edge cases where the specification extends beyond initial assumptions
memory_items.generalization: Verify the complete answer_position bounds before implementing cell-write loops, accounting for all columns and rows mentioned in the task specification

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Formula String Validation
When generating Excel formulas via string concatenation, validate the resulting formula text contains exactly one leading '=' and conforms to Excel syntax before assignment. Test formula strings programmatically (check for duplicate operators, malformed ranges) prior to saving.

### Row-Specific Formulas
For multi-row formula insertion where each output row computes independently, do not apply identical absolute ranges to all cells unless aggregation across rows is intended. Construct row-aware ranges using relative references or dynamically generated range boundaries appropriate to each target cell.

### Complete Output Range Coverage
Always implement cell writes covering the full answer_position range specified in task instructions, verifying both column spans and row limits match the required output dimensions.
